reviews.review_description: So it worked good for just a little bit and then after an update,  it just stopped working now when you click on it it just blinks and says error ￼ and reinstalling doesn’t work
This great app seems to have been renewed as Stellerium VR which is very similar. To repurchase is not too expensive.

reviews.review_title: Not a real Star chart. Only Multimedia.
reviews.review_description: No location or time options. What Star chart is that?

The app works well on meta quest 2 and I was amazed how good the graphics is. Also the movement doesn't make you sick like someone else said. One minus is that the side controller button returns you instantly in the main app menu and my kids frequently pressed it by mistake and then start winging. Also I would like to be able to see more space objects like galaxies, nebulae, maybe even some artificial satellites. All in all, my kids loves it and every day they ask me to see the planets.
Very informative

I like this APP really a lot. Just a couple of improvements can make it a lot better like:
- a completely free of movements in the exploration mode (a must)
- the option to manually zoom in/out in exploration mode (see previous point)
- the option to zoom in/out in the planetarium using the joystick up/down
- the option to set time speed in the planetarium using joystick left/right
- the option to show artificial satellites revolving around earth
- a lot more automatic tours (like exploration of Jupiter satellites, discovering of Apollo 11 landing sites, follow voyager 1/2 flight path, etc.)

I Like the App but i would Like to have the option to land on the planets. I mean I would like to walk and see the sky of the planets. Is it possible? I have found some videos in YouTube but i am sure you could do it better. ☺️

this does not represent the real sky, even though the company claims that it does! if you want a real start chart this is not it, this is a simplistic pseudo star map that is totally off with the positions of the planets, the moon etc… I will return it.

This is the worst astronomical app I have ever used. The Sun never comes out of the southern hemisphere, to bad I live in the northern hemisphere. So the Sun never represents its true location. With everything else is off. Sent an email to the company and no answer. So they are as good as their product. Sorry I bought this. Now to see if I can return.
I guess it is what it says: a star chart. No search possibility. Too basic to be worth anything. Worse than a PC app.
https://www.oculus. com/appreferrals/justin.isafake/1951863938215666/?utm_source=2

Ok. My review, I never leave them anywhere but this is up my alley. For what this is, it’s not bad. I worked with data from Kepler, so this obviously caught my attention. Saw an old review that AU’s where off, they’re fixed now. Could not find anything out of the ordinary, at least from what I saw, but I’m only 30 minutes in. 
Saw another review that stars in background look “cartoonish”. Ok, I get what your saying, I see it too, but the quest is built on a snapdragon 835, level of detail is limited, come on, this is irrelevant. Click on those stars and you get a great star class view in the palm of your hand, with basic info. 
I did notice, somehow when I clicked on Earth, I got population info on US cities. While impressed, this info was off. Boston dose not have 4.8 million. I live in the Philadelphia area now, and while that was correct, I think Philly is 6th in US cities, I clicked on at least 20 US cities that had more. Not really important for something titled Star Chart, but I see how some info could be off. 
Controls while simple are annoying at first until you get a hang of them. Hit the wrong button just once and you come all the way out to the main menu, and have to start all over agin. This is the only reason I didn’t give it a full five stars. 
Again for what it is and what your working with, Star Chart is pretty impressive on the Quest, at 30 minutes max at a time. After 30 minutes, I needed to quit, but it’s definitely an app I’ll come back to again and again. Definitely worth the $9.99 price.

developer_response.body: Hi joro0414. Thanks for your kind review, we are happy you enjoyed the app. We've added the controls issue to our list of things to address in the future. Kind regards, the Star Chart Team.

reviews.review_title: Full of misinformation, but looks nice
reviews.review_description:
Half an hour into exploring this app I noticed lots of incorrect information in the orrery. Earth is not 2.4 au from the Sun and Mars' moons aren't 40,000,000 kms away from Mars. I wasn't even looking for errors, so I can only assume there are lots of mistakes. For an educational app, this isn't good enough.
I sent an email to these guys 2 weeks ago and still haven't heard back, nor have the mistakes been fixed. I gave them the chance to avoid a negative review, but wasn't even acknowledged.
Aside from that, I actually like this app. The graphics are nice and the overall UX is good. Just don't use it as a resource for a school project.

Update: A month later and the simple mistakes still aren't fixed. And I haven't heard any sort of response from the dev, even though they have replied to some reviews. I was hoping to increase my rating by now, but instead I'm decreasing it.

A simple Google search and changing some numbers is all it would take. If the information was accurate, I might rate this app 4 stars.

developer_response.body: Hi Crazy. Thanks for your review, we're going to be reviewing the information again soon, so hopefully will have updated any issues. If you have any specifics you'd like us to address feel free to let us know. Kind regards, the Star Chart Team.

reviews.review_title: Good, But Could Be So Much Better
reviews.review_description:
Update:
It has been 3.5 years since my first review. I checked it out again hoping for an update, but no such luck. It appears that the app is basically abandoned by the developer. There is so much good feedback in the reviews. Could be so good. :( 
--
The port from Gear / Go leaves a lot wanting.  It is basically a Gear / Go quality app, and not really ready for Quest yet.  A great example of this is that you can only ever use one controller at a time.  When you look at your controller, it actually looks like a Go controller in your view, rather than a quest controller.  The buttons are all mislabeled as a result, and in the wrong places.  

This could be so much better, if it took advantage of Quest's features.  Why not let me grab objects and pan, spin, expand, in a natural way, instead of in the awkward way I currently have to do it using only one controller that doesn't even use all the buttons on that controller.

Another complaint is how fake the night sky looks.  This is VR!  Why not give users a place to go to see the night sky as it really looks, instead of making it look so small, confining, and fake.  I would love to stand in a fake field or mountain somewhere and look at the sky, then fly out and explore, using the full power that Quest offers developers.  This app puts you in a small globe that feels like it only extends out a few feet around you.  

I have used some amazing space apps in VR.  Both in Rift and Vive.  They could teach this company a lot. 

Having said all of this, there are pros as well.  It is the only app like it currently available.  It does have some (though not very good) options for teaching my kids the constellations.  It does let you travel through space, (though not very convincingly, and using awkward controls).  However it is a good first step.

Hoping they put out a redo, after this first to market launch, and not simply port their Gear / Go app, but actually create a much more immersive experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. We'll review and see what we can do. Couple of notes - the controller is the Quest controller, but we've removed the sensor ring to stop it interfering with the UI of objects on your controller. The app isn't a Gear / Go port, but rather a "port" of the Rift version - pretty much includes everything there is from that version. We do have use a fully realistic & real-time 3D model of the solar system so when you travel to another planet, you are actually travelling the full distance - at faster than light speeds. But space is *big* (and mostly empty), so although we've found that free navigation is a great idea, it doesn't work well when done to scale - as our app is. Having said that, we appreciate your feedback and will look into what we can do to address these issues.

reviews.review_description: Not much to do, and the stuff that there is to do is just boring. Pretty to look at but boring.

developer_response.body: Hi beachteen1. Thanks for your review, and sorry to hear you found the app boring. Please feel free to suggest how we could make it more interesting. Kind regards, the Star Chart Team.

reviews.review_title: For an app named Star Chart...
reviews.review_description:
...it's not very good at star charts. It's great for the orrery and stuff (Aughra would approve), but the star Chart functionality is weak.
The stars are too big and bright, the Milky Way doesn't look remotely real, the background is too bright, you can't change time or location. It makes it very difficult to plan an observing session, or to just relax and pretend to be looking up at the stars. If anything Google Earth VR provides a better stargazing experience unfortunately.
There has obviously been a lot of love put into this app, but most of it in the flashy, toy-ish, game-ish department. This is all well and good, but I wish the dev would spend more time in making the star chart look more like a real night sky... 

Thanks for making this though, it really the only app available for the Quest that remotely caters to stargazers.

developer_response.body: Hi Cuiv. Thanks for your review, like you said a lot of effort was put into making Star Chart, however sadly we missed the mark with your expectations. We'd be interested in hearing what exactly you would like to be able to achieve with Star Chart in VR, as we will be reviewing new features soon. Kind regards, the Star Chart Team.

reviews.review_description: This app is amazing!! I really like the graphics it looks so real!!

developer_response.body: Hi Bowling45. Thanks for your kind review, we are happy that you enjoyed the app. Kind regards, the Star Chart Team.

reviews.review_description: Maybe you can download the Messier NGC catalog. Into the game and let us search by those objects. Could you also show the pronunciation of the stars under their name off of the Control paddle view.  Let us rotate axis' so we can search and learn the southern constellations.
It doesn't show the stars in my location and there is no way to fix it.

This isn't a star chart... there's big goofy constellation cartoons, but it doesn't tell you the name of a single star (at least as far as I can tell). It does have vomit inducing trips from planet to planet. I'm still pretty motion sick, but that's totally my problem.

I really expected at least one of the modes in this thing to be an actual star chart. Put me sortof in my home state so I can re-create my sky and look up a couple things? maybe? I couldn't get off the equator and found literally 0 useful features in this.

I guess I'd give this 4 stars if the name was Solar System Explorer. the graphics are pretty neat. The controls are weird, but it's pretty thrilling to click a planet and have it in your hand kindof. So, I mean, I had fun for a second, but for $10 I expected a program called Star Chart to have an actual star chart in it.

developer_response.body: Hi Spotted Kangaroo. The app does indeed have a star chart - it's called Sky View mode. Once in this mode you'll see a view of the night sky as it is from your GPS coordinates. The GPS coordinates are detected from your IP address as the Oculus Quest does not support GPS data from your device. Kind regards, the Star Chart Team.
